---
title: "Meeting Notes"
---

Notes from coaching sessions and internal team meetings. Sorted in reverse chronological
order (most recent first).

---

## 2026-05-05 — On-site coaching (final review)

**Participants:** Sendogan Kulakci, Lewis Birrer, Dr. Manuel Dömer

**Topics discussed:**

- Walked through the deployed prototype at data.kulakci.de. Dömer noted the network
  layout is interpretable and the provenance links are a strong feature.
- Entity resolution discussed: Dömer confirmed that flagging it as a limitation and
  planning it post-submission is sufficient for the grade criteria.
- Quarto documentation reviewed; feedback: evaluation section should be more explicit
  about what the betweenness values mean substantively, not just methodologically.
  → Added a "sanity check" note: top-ranked entities align with known key figures.
- Presentation slides to be completed by 2026-05-19.

**Decisions:**

- Submit pipeline + documentation as-is; entity resolution is future work.
- Presentation to be 15 min + 5 min Q&A.

---

## 2026-04-21 — On-site coaching (prototype review)

**Participants:** Sendogan Kulakci, Lewis Birrer, Dr. Manuel Dömer

**Topics discussed:**

- First working prototype demonstrated: Cosmograph WebGL frontend with real network
  data from the 1 000-doc-per-stratum sample.
- Performance was noticeably better than the earlier Streamlit prototype attempt.
  Dömer asked about the tech stack change; team explained WebGL motivation.
- NER quality discussed. Dömer pointed out that OCR noise is expected in this type of
  corpus and that documenting it as a limitation is the right approach rather than
  trying to fix it within the project scope.
- Sidebar provenance links approved as a design feature.

**Decisions:**

- Keep Cosmograph. Update charta note to reflect stack change.
- Add entity resolution as an explicit future-work item in evaluation.qmd.

---

## 2026-03-24 — Concept coaching

**Participants:** Sendogan Kulakci, Lewis Birrer, Dr. Manuel Dömer

**Topics discussed:**

- Research question refined. Earlier version was too broad ("analyse Epstein connections").
  Focused it on structural holes / bridge actors following Dömer's suggestion to ground
  the concept theoretically.
- Tech stack decision: Streamlit initially proposed; team flagged potential scalability
  issues with large graphs. Dömer suggested evaluating D3 or a WebGL option.
  → After the session, team evaluated Cosmograph and decided to use it.
- Dataset confirmed: Nikity/Epstein-Files on HuggingFace. Dömer asked about provenance;
  confirmed the dataset links back to DOJ source URLs.
- Stratified sampling strategy discussed and approved (1 000 docs per stratum,
  file_type == pdf, text_content not null).

**Decisions:**

- Research question finalised (see Project Charta).
- Switch from Streamlit to React + Cosmograph WebGL.
- Stratified sample as the primary dataset with SAMPLE_SIZE as a config variable.

---

## 2026-03-03 — Internal kick-off meeting

**Participants:** Sendogan Kulakci, Lewis Birrer

**Topics discussed:**

- Reviewed the available dataset options. Decided on Nikity/Epstein-Files because:
  (1) publicly available and legally unambiguous (DOJ provenance),
  (2) pre-extracted text reduces preprocessing overhead,
  (3) the subject matter is well-suited to network analysis (named individuals,
      institutions, documented relationships).
- Discussed scope constraints (2–3 hours/week each, 8–10 weeks). Agreed to use a
  sampled subset rather than the full 4.1 M-row corpus.
- Assigned roles: Sendogan leads Python pipeline; Lewis leads frontend; shared
  documentation responsibility.
- Repository set up from the dataviz-project-template.

**Decisions:**

- Dataset: Nikity/Epstein-Files.
- Architecture: Python NER pipeline → network JSON → React WebGL frontend.
- Documentation: Quarto on GitHub Pages (this site).
